name: Basic Pancakes
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift dry ingredients into a bowl.
2. Stir in liquid ingredients with a wire whisk or spoon until mixed, but do not overmix.
3. Rub the griddle or frying pan with oil.
4. Heat until water droplets evaporate when dropped from your hand.
5. Scoop about 1/2 cup (or 1 large mixing spoon) batter for each pancake.
6. Cook until bubbled all over and brown on the bottom.
7. Flip pancakes and cook until brown on the other side.
8. Keep the griddle very hot at all times.
Variations:
1. Substitute 1 cup whole wheat flour for white flour.
2. Substitute buttermilk for whole milk and 1 tsp baking soda for 1/2 tsp baking powder.
3. Add pecan or walnut pieces (or blueberries) to the dry ingredients to prevent them from sinking.
4. Increase the number of eggs for a thinner, crepe-like texture. Top with sliced apples, brown sugar, and cinnamon before folding in half.
